compiled_state.title: sprint-list-epics.sh triggers rebase conflict on tickets branch when local is behind remote
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description: When running .claude/scripts/dso sprint-list-epics.sh --max-children=0, the script triggers an interactive rebase on the tickets orphan branch. If the local tickets branch is behind origin/tickets, the rebase hits a merge conflict on .gitignore (both added), leaving the tickets worktree in a broken rebase state. This blocks all subsequent ticket CLI operations (create, list, etc.) until manually resolved. Observed during /dso:brainstorm Phase 1 Step 1. Root cause: the ticket CLI auto-rebases the local tickets branch onto origin/tickets, but does not handle .gitignore conflicts gracefully. Workaround: manually resolve the conflict in .tickets-tracker/ and run git rebase --continue.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 3 (INTERMEDIATE). severity=1, complexity=1, environment=1, intermittent=0.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). Rebase conflict is a genuine defect introduced by commit b0c235b8 when fixing ddd3-d2a6. The tickets branch architecture guarantees conflict-free event files but .gitignore is not an event file.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: SAFEGUARD APPROVED: user approved editing ticket scripts. Fix: add stale rebase detection/abort to ticket-init.sh and replace pull --rebase with safer sync.
comments.author: Test
